title: atriplex argentea var. hillmanii
intro: Atriplex argentea var. hillmanii, also known as Hillman's saltbush, is a variant of the Atriplex argentea plant, a type of shrub that is native to Australia and is characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and ability to tolerate saline conditions. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Hillman's saltbush include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Hillman's saltbush include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or mixing them with other herbs to create a medicinal paste.
